concept.shortdesc: Sit in the operator seat and fasten the seatbelt. Ensure the parking brake is applied

conbody.p: Sit in the operator seat and fasten the seatbelt. Ensure the parking brake is applied before starting the engine.
Place the directional control lever in NEUTRAL. Depress the brake pedal and hold.
Turn the ignition key to the ON position. Allow the glow plug indicator light to go out before starting in cold conditions.
Turn the key to START. Release the key when the engine starts. Do not crank the engine for more than 30 seconds continuously.
Allow the engine to warm up at low idle for 3–5 minutes before beginning operation. Monitor all gauges during warm-up.
If the engine does not start after two attempts, consult the troubleshooting section or contact a qualified technician.
Page 395 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
